hero:
# Google Play Store Analytics

**App-market performance analysis · Python · SQL · Scikit-learn · Tableau**

## Hero Overview

An analysis of 2.5M+ Google Play Store app records, using chi-square feature engineering to surface the KPIs most correlated with market fit and modeling category-level growth trends.

Results shipped as Tableau dashboards translating statistical signal into development-strategy recommendations for product and marketing teams.

points.evidence: Analyzed 2.5M+ Play Store app records with Python and SQL, structuring category-level slices for performance analysis.
Conducted chi-square feature engineering across 10+ attributes (size, gamification, utility) to surface market-fit KPIs.
Modeled app-performance and category growth with Scikit-learn, generating reproducible benchmarks for strategy.
Built 3 Tableau dashboards translating engagement signals into stakeholder-ready views and 3 strategy recommendations.

network_graph.title: Technology Capability Network
network_graph.description: Maps the project's core techniques to the tools used to implement them.

storyline:
# Storyline

## Context

Product and marketing teams needed to know which app attributes actually correlate with market fit across a noisy 2.5M-record store catalog.

## Workflow

Category-level data slicing → chi-square feature engineering → trend modeling → Tableau dashboards → strategy recommendations.

## Technical Implementation

- **Analysis:** 2.5M+ records with Pandas/NumPy and SQL, structured into category slices.
- **Features:** chi-square testing across 10+ attributes (size, gamification, utility) to rank market-fit KPIs.
- **Modeling:** Scikit-learn trend models producing reproducible category-growth benchmarks.
- **Dashboards:** 3 Tableau views translating signals for non-technical teams.

## Outcomes

Turned statistical signal into 3 development-strategy recommendations aligned to product-team decision questions.
